I love🍁 the app.! The app is so much faster ✔️download. Not to mention being unable to use the app when it’s syncing because it literally stops responding until it’s done.!Thank you so much to everyone algorithms. You’d stop an entry and it’d be several seconds before it’d actually stop and those extra seconds are added to the entry. This syncing happens pretty much every time you open the app, start, stop, or edit an entry so it was a real pain to use. But now download faster and more stable.

INDmoney bugs: Txn times show AEDT instead of IST. Indpay UPI scans ask for PIN then error out, requiring a force close. Critical Flaw: If an Indpay txn hangs due to low signal, the next payment pushes the old pending amount instead of the new one (e.g., sent 30k medical bill instead of 60). Disabling UPI till refunded. Feature Requests: Add separate tax-saving fund card (like Groww). Improve EPFO for corporate private PF trusts (HCL/TCS) or add manual entry (like Artos). Add Wi-Fi tablet suppot

INDmoney is one of the best finance management apps I have used. It provides a clean and user-friendly interface to track investments, mutual funds, stocks, EPF, loans, and overall net worth in one place. The app makes it easy to monitor financial growth with detailed insights and reports. The U.S. stock investing feature is smooth, and account aggregation helps users get a complete picture of their finances. Notifications and reminders are useful without being overwhelming.

In India, stock market investments are regulated by SEBI, and investors have a regulatory authority to approach if any fraud occurs. Similarly, since we are investing in U.S. stocks through your platform, I would like to understand what investor protection mechanisms are in place. If any fraud, platform issue, or loss due to operational failure occurs, whom should investors approach for assistance and recovery of their invested funds? Which regulatory authority oversees such matters?
